name: SQL In HTTP Handler
summary: Avoid placing direct SQL or persistence-specific query logic inside HTTP handlers.

antiPatterns: Do not open database transactions or execute SQL directly from an Axum handler when an application use case should own the operation.
Do not let HTTP route shape dictate persistence model shape.
checks: Persistence code is behind an application or infrastructure boundary, not inline in the handler.

blocks.markdown: Direct persistence work in an HTTP handler couples protocol, transaction, query, and behavior decisions. That makes reuse and focused tests harder.

blocks.reason: Thin handler guidance avoids persistence leakage.
